raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The unilateral power to authorize a nuclear first-strike should not rest with a single individual.",
    "The proposed legislation HR 669 would prohibit the President from unilaterally authorizing the deployment of nuclear weapons in an offensive attack unless Congress provides express authorization.",
    "HR 669 allows the President to respond in-kind to a nuclear attack on the United States.",
    "The preeminent science and security board of the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists moved the minute hand of the Doomsday Clock 30 seconds closer to catastrophe.",
    "The current time on the Doomsday Clock is two minutes to midnight, which is as close as it was at the height of the Cold War in 1953.",
    "The ability to wield nuclear power should be shared by the branches of the federal government rather than being solely in the hands of the President.",
    "The rhetoric of the current President regarding nuclear war is concerning and should not come from a Commander-in-Chief.",
    "HR 669 aims to significantly reduce the threat of nuclear confrontation."
  ]
}
```
